"For IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII, being part of this global community is more than a milestone — it is a strategic commitment." — Ionuț Iablonschi, Partner
"As members of Marcalliance, we explore new paths for our clients, offering cross-border legal services with the confidence of working alongside trusted partners worldwide." — Corina Iablonschi, Partner

The firm returned from the Annual Meeting energised, inspired, and ready to build lasting bridges within the alliance and deliver substantial value to clients across jurisdictions worldwide.

title: IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII Advises CCP Cooperatief on the Acquisition of a Logistics Complex in Ștefăneștii de Jos, Romania
excerpt: IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII served as legal advisor to Cultivate Capital Partners Fund Cooperatief U.A. for a phased acquisition of a logistics complex valued at approximately €7,650,000.
body:
IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII served as legal advisor to Cultivate Capital Partners Fund Cooperatief U.A. ("CCP Cooperatief") for the phased acquisition of a logistics complex in Ștefăneștii de Jos, Romania.

Key Transaction Details

  • Type: Phased acquisition
  • Value: Approximately €7,650,000
  • Industry: Real Estate – Logistics
  • Status: First phase completed

CCP Cooperatief, founded in 2024 in the Netherlands, focuses on sustainable growth and innovation, investing in both emerging and established markets. In Romania, it operates through CCP Log Holding S.R.L., targeting investments in non-residential property. The initial transaction comprised the purchase of a 13,000 square metre logistics warehouse, fully leased, alongside 31,000 square metres of land.

IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII's role encompassed due diligence, structuring the transaction, negotiating documents, and managing the foreign investment notification process with the relevant Romanian authorities. The team was spearheaded by Amalia Rapan (Of Counsel) and Ionuț Iablonschi (Partner) for the acquisition and negotiation, with Remus Ene (Partner) leading on the foreign investment notification.

"We are thrilled to support CCP Cooperatief in this pivotal transaction, reinforcing their commitment to expanding their asset portfolio in Romania." — Amalia Rapan, Of Counsel
"This transaction demanded a bespoke approach, aligning with our principles of integrated service, transparent communication, and a strategic mindset." — Ionuț Iablonschi, Partner
"Thanks to Amalia and Ionut, the transaction was not only seamless but also reassuring." — Emre Ozgen, Founder, Cultivate Capital Partners

The upcoming acquisition phase is scheduled for April 2025, focusing on acquiring the other buildings at the Ștefăneștii de Jos site.

Joining this active community aligns with our commitment to represent our clients' interests in pertinent business environments. We value the opportunity to engage with professionals focused on addressing concrete challenges and developing innovative solutions.

At IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII, we prioritise practicality and excellence while maintaining a human-centred approach. By integrating exceptional local knowledge with exposure to international best practices, we aim to offer comprehensive services that support our clients' business objectives. title: The RO e-Transport System in the Light of the Sanctioning System Applicable Starting with July 1, 2024. Possible Sanctions and Remedies.
excerpt: A comprehensive analysis of Romania's RO e-Transport system and the sanctioning regime implemented from July 1, 2024 — by Corina Iablonschi and Gabriel Gheorghe (Soter & Partners).
body:
By Corina Iablonschi, Partner, IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII & Gabriel Gheorghe, Associate Tax Partner, SOTER & PARTNERS

Overview of the System

The RO e-Transport system was designed as "a mechanism to combat fraud and tax evasion, by preventing concealment in goods commercialisation on national territory." Initially monitoring only high-risk fiscal transports, Emergency Ordinance 115/2023 expanded it to include international road transport declarations. The system identifies goods batches through a unique UIT code and is administered by Romania's Ministry of Finance through the National Fiscal Administration Agency and the National Centre for Financial Information.

Implementation Challenges

Operational procedures appeared extremely late — Order 1337/2024, establishing the unified procedure for both domestic high-risk and international transport declarations, was published on June 27, 2024, only one business day before enforcement began on July 1. This created significant compliance difficulties for operators who had no adequate time to adapt.

Sanctioning Framework (post-July 1, 2024)

Two categories of sanctions exist:

  • Single penalties (fines only) under Articles 131(c) and (e): fines of 10,000–100,000 RON
  • Double sanctions (fines + forfeiture) under Articles 131(a) and (b): fines plus confiscation of the goods' value

A critical exception introduced by Emergency Ordinance 43/2024 exempts complementary confiscation when verification occurs after transport completion and accounting entry. Unlike standard contraventions, violators cannot pay half the minimum fine within 15 days — a derogation from Government Ordinance 2/2001. Emergency Ordinance 87/2024 also postponed sanctions until January 1, 2025 exclusively for AEO-certified businesses under EU Regulation 952/2013.

Criticism and Concerns

The authors highlight significant practical problems: verbal reports often issued after transport completion, denying operators the opportunity for explanation; sanctions described as "rather a hunt for errors than for evaders," disproportionately affecting honest businesses; confiscation applied as "the rule" rather than the exception. The cumulative effect has been "disastrous results for taxpayers," with ongoing litigations exceeding half a million euros.

By comparison, Hungary's EKAER system (implemented since 2015) requires authorities to "weigh all circumstances in each case," with penalties ranging 0–40% based on behavioural evaluation and typical fines approximating 100,000 HUF (~250 euros) — a starkly different approach to proportionality.

Remedies and Legal Recourse

Sanctioned operators may file contraventional complaints within 15 days of receiving the violation notice, which automatically suspends both principal and complementary sanctions — described by the authors as "an exceptionally practical remedy" given the substantial penalty amounts. Until legislative intervention occurs, courts reviewing complaints bear responsibility for assessing sanction proportionality, with authority to annul penalties, replace fines with warnings, and eliminate complementary confiscation.

The authors emphasise the importance of seeking specialist legal advice promptly if sanctions are received, given the strict 15-day filing window.

title: WHO ARE WE? IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII Talents in the Spotlight — Edit Vesser
excerpt: Edit Vesser, Strategic Projects Director, shares the eight critical professional skills she has identified as essential for success in any field.
body:
We are proud to introduce a series spotlighting the professionals behind IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII. In this edition, we feature Edit Vesser, Strategic Projects Director.

Edit emphasises that "even if you're not in a formal leadership role, possessing leadership qualities such as initiative, accountability, and the ability to motivate others can set you apart."

She identifies eight critical skills for professional success across all fields:

  1. Communication — conveying ideas clearly and active listening
  2. Problem-solving — analysing situations and developing practical solutions
  3. Teamwork and Collaboration — working effectively with colleagues
  4. Time Management — prioritising tasks and meeting deadlines
  5. Adaptability — adjusting to workplace changes and industry trends
  6. Leadership — demonstrating initiative and accountability
  7. Emotional Intelligence — managing emotions and empathising with others
  8. Continuous Learning — staying current with industry knowledge

Edit developed these skills through formal education, practical experience, mentorship, and reflective self-improvement — learning from both successes and failures throughout her career.

title: WHO ARE WE? IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII Talents in the Spotlight — Corina Iablonschi
excerpt: Corina Iablonschi, Partner, shares her motivation for pursuing law and the values that shape her approach to client service.
body:
We continue our series spotlighting the professionals behind IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚIEI. In this edition, we feature Corina Iablonschi, Partner.

"My deep belief that great things are born from effort and passion inspired me to become a lawyer." — Corina Iablonschi, Partner

Corina describes her approach as proactive and detail-oriented, with a focus on protecting client interests through careful attention to every matter she handles. She emphasises her commitment to serving others and making a constructive impact.

Her philosophy centres on the idea that precision and dedication — not shortcuts — are what deliver real value to clients in complex legal situations.

title: Key legal and fiscal topics to be explored during an upcoming seminar
excerpt: Corina Iablonschi and Ionuț Iablonschi will serve as speakers at a legal and fiscal seminar hosted by TAX ADVISORS in Sibiu, Romania on June 7–8, 2024.
body:
Iablonschi & Asociații announced that two of its principals — Corina Iablonschi and Ionuț Iablonschi — will serve as speakers at a legal news seminar hosted by TAX ADVISORS in Sibiu, Romania.

The June 7–8, 2024 event will feature presentations addressing fiscal and accounting matters relevant to the business sector.

The duo's specific contribution will focus on: "Tax inspection. Challenging tax administrative acts. The importance of cooperation between tax consultant and lawyer" — examining critical legal and fiscal considerations companies should prioritise in the near term.

The seminar agenda encompasses multiple presentations on fiscal and accounting topics of significance to the business community.

title: Corina Iablonschi and Edit Vesser join PWN Romania
excerpt: Corina Iablonschi and Edit Vesser have joined Professional Women's Network Romania, part of PWN Global — a leadership development platform for professional women across 24 cities.
body:
Corina Iablonschi and Edit Vesser have joined Professional Women's Network Romania (PWN Romania), part of PWN Global — a network and leadership development platform for professional women from various sectors and industries.

PWN Global has more than 3,500 members of over 90 nationalities. This volunteer-led organisation hosts over 600 events per year in 24 different cities.

"The sense of community represents the drive to initiate or contribute to initiatives that aim to enhance or support personal development. In my opinion, this must be a lifelong objective, whether in a professional context, or within our close or extended family. With PWN Romania, I believe that I will be able to successfully pursue this personal objective and guide others to do so while enjoying and nurturing the sense of community that the organisation promotes." — Corina Iablonschi, Partner

title: IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII Joins Marcalliance, Strengthening Its Global Network and Eastern European Presence
excerpt: IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII announced its appointment as Romania's representative within Marcalliance, a global network of independent law and tax firms operating across 28 countries.
body:
IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII is proud to announce its appointment as a Romanian member of Marcalliance, a distinguished international network of independent law and tax firms with presence in 28 countries.

With this incorporation, Marcalliance reinforces its position in Eastern Europe, expanding its ability to provide member firms' clients with seamless assistance in one of the region's strong-performing economies. This strategic membership reinforces IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII's vision of delivering comprehensive, high-quality, and integrated legal and tax solutions, while opening access for international and domestic corporations to nuanced, tailored professional attention with a refreshing globalized perspective.

"We are glad to welcome IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII to our Alliance and therefore, counting on such a reliable legal team player in Romania. Eastern Europe is a recipient of foreign investments that undoubtedly require specific legal advice from teams that are used to dealing with international clients." — Dorothée De Bernis, President, Marcalliance

Founded by seasoned professionals with extensive experience in top-tier law firms and Big Four environments, IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII specialises in a wide range of legal areas, including tax, dispute resolution, corporate law, mergers and acquisitions, real estate, and regulatory matters (competition law, data privacy, energy and natural resources). The firm is recognised for its commitment to delivering commercially viable solutions across industries, with a particular focus on agribusiness, FMCG, energy, technology, and real estate.

"Joining Marcalliance is a crucial milestone for our firm, allowing us to enhance our ability to serve clients operating across multiple jurisdictions. We are excited about collaborating with Marcalliance's esteemed members, sharing expertise, and contributing to a global network that shares our values of integrity, quality, and innovation. This partnership will empower us to better support our clients' international ambitions and ensure consistent, reliable service wherever their business takes them." — Ionuț Iablonschi, Founder & Partner

Through its integration with Marcalliance, IABLONSCHI & ASOCIAȚII aims to uphold the network's high standards, contribute to its global expertise, and leverage its resources to create new opportunities for clients both in Romania and internationally.
